title: League of Legends Patch 26.14 Notes
overview: Patch 26.14 is a broad balance pass timed to T1's championship celebration: a handful of mid-lane mages and enchanters get tuned, a few bruisers and marksmen shift, and the jungle sees a Blue Buff and Azir rework-adjacent quality-of-life change alongside a big buff-bar UI cleanup.
summary: Seraphine, Senna, and Nami enchanters get reshuffled — two nerfed, one buffed.
Corki, Mordekaiser, and Azir get buffed while Garen, Jayce, and Locke take nerfs.
Blue Buff now scales ability haste with level, and the buff bar was heavily decluttered.
Hextech Rocketbelt, Immortal Path, and Protoplasm Harness all got weaker.

champions.summary: Soldiers now proc on-hit runes at full value and R shoves jungle camps.
champions.details: W soldiers apply Scorch, Liandry's, Blackfire Torch, and Hextech Alternator at 100% instead of 50%, Conqueror gets 2 stacks, and R now knocks back jungle monsters — so your poke does real damage and you can wall off objectives.
champions.name: Azir

champions.summary: More AD growth and faster rocket recharge from autos.
champions.details: Base AD growth 2 → 2.5 and R rocket recharge from attacks improved to 2-6s from 2-4s, meaning more Big One rockets up over the course of a game.
champions.name: Corki

champions.summary: Stronger E damage and a bigger stat steal on the ult.
champions.details: E scales to 60/80/100/120/140 (+45% AP) and Realm of Death steals 13% of stats instead of 10%, so every 1v1 death realm swings harder in your favor.
champions.name: Mordekaiser

champions.summary: E now hits noticeably harder at higher ranks.
champions.details: Tidecaller's Blessing damage per hit rises to 20/35/50/65/80 from 20/30/40/50/60, adding real trade damage to your empowered auto-attacks.
champions.name: Nami

champions.details: Base AD growth 2.5 → 3, giving her steadier scaling auto damage through the game.
champions.name: Yunara

champions.summary: Demacian Justice deals less flat true damage.
champions.details: R base true damage drops to 125/200/275 from 150/250/350 (missing-health scaling unchanged), so your execute needs the enemy lower before it kills.
champions.name: Garen

champions.summary: Less passive move speed and a weaker hammer-form ult.
champions.details: Hextech Capacitor move speed 40 → 30 and Mercury Hammer R armor/MR down to 5/12/19/26, making him squishier and slipperier in melee form.
champions.name: Jayce

champions.summary: Q and W damage and uptime cut across the board.
champions.details: Q recast window 5s → 4s with lower base and mark damage, and W grey-health cap reduced to 40/70/100/130/160, so his poke-and-sustain pattern is meaningfully weaker.
champions.name: Locke

champions.summary: Lower crit modifier and weaker Q utility scaling.
champions.details: Passive crit damage modifier 85% → 80% and Q's AP slow ratio (7% per 100 AP) and heal ratio (35%) both drop, taming her scaling as an AP-leaning support carry.
champions.name: Senna

champions.summary: Q poke scales less with AP.
champions.details: High Note AP ratio 50% → 40%, cutting into her lane poke and mid/late damage output.
champions.name: Seraphine

items.summary: Rebuilt with more health, less AP, and a longer dash cooldown.
items.details: Now builds from Hextech Alternator + Kindlegem + Ruby Crystal, with AP dropped to 60, health up to 350, and active cooldown lengthened to 50s — tankier but a weaker burst dash.
items.name: Hextech Rocketbelt

items.summary: Less bonus damage above half HP and less healing below it.
items.details: Above-50% damage bonus 5% → 4% and below-50% healing/shielding/regen bonus 15% → 12%, softening its bruiser sustain.
items.name: Immortal Path

items.summary: Pricier with a lower health/healing floor.
items.details: Cost 2500 → 2600 and both bonus health and healing now start at 100 instead of 200, making early purchases less efficient.
items.name: Protoplasm Harness

systems.summary: Blue Buff now grants scaling ability haste.
systems.details: Blue Buff ability haste scales to 10/15/20 at levels 1/6/11 instead of a flat 10, giving AP junglers and mid laners more spell uptime as the game goes on.

systems.summary: Major buff-bar cleanup and clearer remake votes.
systems.details: Redundant and ultra-short buffs are hidden, item buffs moved into the inventory display, and remake votes now start automatically with chat explaining who triggered them — so you can actually read your status bar in a fight.

systems.summary: Caitlyn Headshot no longer misfires in brush.
systems.details: A fix stops Caitlyn's Headshot from misfiring in brush, restoring her intended lane trading pattern.
